attempt: Transfer TICKET63's rule: a stable separator must become an explicit automaton table with transition closure and collision tests before it can support any theorem claim.

bounded_result.automaton_gate: zero-kernel separator states need an explicit height-transition automaton
bounded_result.candidate_theorem: A finite zero-kernel automaton covers every height lift with positivity-preserving transitions, or a height collision produces an off-critical obstruction packet.
bounded_result.counterexample_target: two height lifts with the same zero-kernel automaton state but conflicting positivity labels
bounded_result.frontier_rule: The next useful artifact is an explicit state table plus a proof of closure or a first collision. Heuristic separator stability is not enough.

obstruction: This is a transfer discipline, not a solution. It raises the target from bounded separator survival to automaton closure.
candidate_theorem: A finite zero-kernel automaton covers every height lift with positivity-preserving transitions, or a height collision produces an off-critical obstruction packet.
next_experiment: Build the problem-specific automaton table and quotient/collision audit.
claim_boundary: No riemann proof and no certified riemann counterexample.
